metadata.methodology: Expert knowledge + AWS best practices + compliance context

aws.guardduty.enabled.expert_notes: GuardDuty detector enabled in all regions satisfies 'enabled' requirement. This is the core check.

aws.guardduty.centrally_managed.expert_notes: Requires checking AWS Organizations delegated administrator for GuardDuty. No existing rule covers this organizational-level check.

aws.guardduty.eks_audit_log_enabled.expert_notes: This checks if GuardDuty EKS Protection is enabled for EKS audit log monitoring. Specific feature check not in rule_list. Alternatively, could be actual EKS control plane audit logging to CloudWatch.

aws.guardduty.no_high_severity_findings.expert_notes: Posture check requiring GuardDuty GetFindings API call to query for HIGH/CRITICAL severity findings. This is runtime compliance, not configuration.

aws.guardduty.security_center_enabled.expert_notes: Likely refers to basic GuardDuty detector enabled. 'Security Center' might be generic term or cross-cloud contamination. Should verify compliance requirement context.

aws.guardduty.vulnerability_assessment_e.expert_notes: GuardDuty does threat detection, not vulnerability assessment. This might be: (1) Amazon Inspector, (2) GuardDuty Runtime Monitoring, or (3) GuardDuty Malware Protection. Requires requirement clarification.

aws.cloudtrail.multi_region_enabled.expert_notes: CloudTrail multi-region trail = flow_logs_enabled covering all regions. Core audit requirement.

aws.cloudtrail.cloudwatch_logging_enable.expert_notes: CloudTrail logs sent to CloudWatch Logs. flow_logs_enabled covers this integration.

aws.cloudtrail.log_file_validation_enabl.expert_notes: CloudTrail log file integrity validation using digest files. Specific feature check not in rule_list. Requires checking trail.LogFileValidationEnabled parameter.

aws.cloudtrail.kms_encryption_enabled.expert_notes: CloudTrail logs encrypted with KMS. logs_centralized_and_encrypted covers this.

aws.cloudtrail.s3_dataevents_read_enable.expert_notes: S3 data event logging (ReadObject, GetObject). When CloudTrail is configured with S3 data events, flow_logs_enabled covers this.

aws.cloudtrail.s3_dataevents_write_enabl.expert_notes: S3 data event logging (PutObject, DeleteObject). When CloudTrail is configured with S3 data events, flow_logs_enabled covers this.

aws.cloudtrail.bucket.access_logging_ena.expert_notes: S3 bucket access logging for the CloudTrail bucket itself. Covered by flow_logs configuration.

aws.cloudtrail.bucket.is_not_publicly_ac.expert_notes: Check if CloudTrail S3 bucket has public access blocked. This is actually an S3 bucket check, not CloudTrail config. Should check bucket policy and ACLs.

cloudtrail._note_metric_filters: Remaining 20 CloudTrail functions are CloudWatch metric filters and alarms - all need development as they're separate from CloudTrail trail configuration

iam.service_description: AWS IAM - Identity and Access Management

aws.iam.password_policy_minimum_length_1.expert_notes: Check IAM password policy MinimumPasswordLength >= 14. Requires GetAccountPasswordPolicy API. Not a resource check but account-level policy check.

aws.iam.policy_no_administrative_privile.expert_notes: Check IAM policies don't grant admin permissions (Action: *, Resource: *). Requires policy document analysis across all policies. Complex check.

aws.iam.no_root_access_key.expert_notes: Check root account has no access keys. Requires GetAccountSummary or credential report analysis for root user.

aws.iam.user_accesskey_unused.expert_notes: Check for IAM users with unused access keys (not used in 90+ days). Requires GetAccessKeyLastUsed API analysis.

aws.iam.user_console_access_unused.expert_notes: Check for IAM users with unused console access. Requires credential report or GetUser password last used analysis.

aws.iam.user_mfa_enabled.expert_notes: Check IAM users have MFA enabled. Requires ListMFADevices for each user.

aws.iam.root_mfa_enabled.expert_notes: Check root account has MFA enabled. Requires GetAccountSummary or credential report for root user MFA status.

iam._note: IAM requires 44 compliance functions. Most are specific policy analysis or credential checks that need dedicated development.

aws.s3.bucket_encryption_enabled.expert_notes: S3 bucket default encryption enabled (AES256 or KMS). Direct mapping.

aws.s3.bucket_versioning_enabled.expert_notes: S3 bucket versioning enabled. Direct 1:1 mapping.

aws.s3.bucket_server_access_logging_enab.expert_notes: S3 bucket access logging captured by CloudTrail S3 data events when enabled. Per CloudTrail principle.

aws.s3.bucket_public_access.expert_notes: S3 bucket public access block settings. Checks all 4 block public access settings.

aws.s3.bucket_secure_transport_policy.expert_notes: S3 bucket policy requires SSL/TLS (aws:SecureTransport). enforce_ssl rule checks this.

aws.s3.bucket_policy_public_write_access.expert_notes: S3 bucket policy doesn't allow public write. Checks for Principal: '*' with Put*/Delete* actions.

aws.s3.account.level_public_access_block.expert_notes: Account-level public access block. Bucket-level rule can serve as proxy, though technically different scope. May need account-level check development.

aws.s3.bucket_kms_encryption.expert_notes: S3 bucket encrypted with customer-managed KMS key. CMEK rules check this.

s3._note: S3 has 9/16 mapped. Remaining are specific configurations or cross-account checks.

aws.ec2.instance_no_public_ip.expert_notes: EC2 instance has no public IP address. Direct 1:1 mapping.

aws.ec2.instance_imdsv2_enabled.expert_notes: EC2 instance requires IMDSv2 (HttpTokens: required). metadata_v2_only checks this security improvement.

aws.ec2.ebs_public_snapshot.expert_notes: EBS snapshots are not public. snapshots_not_public checks createVolumePermission.

aws.ec2.ebs_snapshots_encrypted.expert_notes: EBS snapshots are encrypted. Direct check of snapshot Encrypted attribute.

aws.ec2.instance_managed_by_ssm.expert_notes: Check if EC2 instance is managed by AWS Systems Manager. Requires checking SSM managed instances list. Not in rule_list.

aws.ec2.instance_profile_attached.expert_notes: EC2 instance has IAM instance profile attached. iam_profile_attached checks IamInstanceProfile.

aws.ec2.instance_older_than_specific_day.expert_notes: Check instance age > X days. Requires comparing LaunchTime with current date. Age-based compliance check.

aws.ec2.ebs_default_encryption.expert_notes: Account-level EBS encryption by default enabled (GetEbsEncryptionByDefault). Different from individual volume encryption.

aws.ec2.ebs_volume_encryption.expert_notes: Individual EBS volume encrypted. Checks volume Encrypted attribute.

aws.ec2.networkacl_allow_ingress_any_por.expert_notes: Check if Network ACL allows ingress from anywhere on any port (0.0.0.0/0). Security misconfiguration check.

aws.ec2.instance_public_ip.expert_notes: Same as instance_no_public_ip - checks PublicIpAddress is null.

ec2._note: EC2 has 81 compliance functions. Most are specific security configurations. 11/81 mapped, 70 need development.

aws.rds.instance_no_public_access.expert_notes: RDS instance PubliclyAccessible = false. Direct 1:1 mapping.

aws.rds.instance_backup_enabled.expert_notes: RDS automated backups enabled (BackupRetentionPeriod > 0). Direct mapping.

aws.rds.instance_storage_encrypted.expert_notes: RDS instance StorageEncrypted = true. Direct mapping.

aws.rds.instance_multi_az.expert_notes: RDS Multi-AZ deployment. high_availability_enabled checks MultiAZ attribute.

aws.rds.instance_deletion_protection.expert_notes: RDS deletion protection. Direct mapping to DeletionProtection attribute.

aws.rds.instance_integration_cloudwatch_.expert_notes: RDS audit logs to CloudWatch. Per CloudTrail principle: CloudTrail captures RDS API calls. For DB engine logs (MySQL slow query, etc.), requires separate RDS log export config.

aws.rds.instance_enhanced_monitoring_ena.expert_notes: RDS Enhanced Monitoring (OS metrics). Checks MonitoringInterval > 0 and MonitoringRoleArn configured.

aws.rds.snapshots_public_access.expert_notes: Check RDS snapshots are not public. Requires DescribeDBSnapshotAttributes for restore permissions.

aws.rds.snapshots_encrypted.expert_notes: RDS snapshot encrypted. snapshot.storage_encrypted checks snapshot Encrypted attribute.

aws.rds.backup_retention_period.expert_notes: Check RDS backup retention period >= minimum days (usually 7 or 30). Needs BackupRetentionPeriod value check.

rds._note: RDS has 10/27 mapped. Core security checks covered, but monitoring and backup retention specifics need development.

aws.kms.cmk_rotation_enabled.expert_notes: KMS key automatic rotation enabled. Direct mapping to KeyRotationEnabled.

aws.kms.key_not_publicly_accessible.expert_notes: KMS key policy doesn't allow public access. no_public_principals checks for Principal: '*' or aws:PrincipalOrgID wildcards.

aws.kms.key_not_scheduled_for_deletion.expert_notes: KMS key not in PendingDeletion state. pending_deletion_status checks KeyState.

aws.kms.kms_key_expiration_set.expert_notes: KMS keys can't expire (unlike certificates). This might be checking ValidTo for imported key material, which is rare. May need clarification.

aws.kms.key_lifecycle_policy.expert_notes: KMS doesn't have lifecycle policies (that's S3/Glacier concept). Might mean key rotation policy or key deletion schedule. Needs clarification.

aws.kms.cmk_rotation_compliance_check.expert_notes: Another alias for rotation enabled check.

aws.kms.crypto_key_iam_policy_public_acc.expert_notes: Same as key_not_publicly_accessible - checks key policy.

kms._note: KMS 9/9 mapped (100%). KMS has simple, well-defined checks that align well with rule_list.

aws.cloudwatch.log_group_retention.expert_notes: CloudWatch Logs retention period >= minimum (usually 90 or 365 days). Direct mapping.

aws.cloudwatch.log_group_kms_encryption_.expert_notes: CloudWatch Logs encrypted with KMS. encryption_at_rest_enabled checks KmsKeyId on log group.

aws.cloudwatch.changes_to_vpcs_alarm_con.expert_notes: CloudWatch metric filter and alarm for VPC changes (CreateVpc, DeleteVpc, etc.). Requires checking metric filters + alarms exist for specific API patterns. Complex check.

aws.cloudwatch.changes_to_network_acls_a.expert_notes: CloudWatch metric filter and alarm for Network ACL changes. Needs metric filter pattern matching.

cloudwatch._note: CloudWatch has 14/25 mapped. Basic logging configs mapped. Metric filter/alarm checks (CIS benchmarks) need development.

vpc.service_description: AWS VPC - Virtual Private Cloud networking

aws.vpc.flow_logs_enabled.expert_notes: VPC Flow Logs enabled for VPC. Direct mapping to flow_logging_enabled.

aws.vpc.network_acl_unused.expert_notes: Identify unused Network ACLs (not associated with subnets). Requires association analysis.

aws.vpc.default_security_group_restricts.expert_notes: Default security group has no inbound/outbound rules. CIS benchmark check.

vpc._note: VPC has limited mappings. Most VPC compliance functions are network security policy checks that need development.

aws.lambda.function_restrict_public_acce.expert_notes: Lambda function not publicly accessible via resource policy. Direct mapping.

aws.lambda.function_url_public.expert_notes: Lambda function URL requires authentication (not AWS_IAM_NONE). authentication_required checks AuthType.

aws.lambda.function_inside_vpc.expert_notes: Lambda function runs inside VPC (has VpcConfig). Direct mapping.

aws.lambda.function_url_cors_policy.expert_notes: Lambda function URL has proper CORS policy. Requires checking Cors configuration on function URL.

lambda._note: Lambda has 4/16 mapped. Core access checks covered.

aws.backup_plan_min_retention.expert_notes: Backup plan has minimum retention period. Direct mapping to retention_days check.

aws.backup.recovery_point_encrypted.expert_notes: Backup recovery points are encrypted. volumes_encrypted checks this.

aws.backup.vault_encryption_enabled.expert_notes: Backup vault encrypted with KMS. Direct mapping.

aws.backup.vaults_encrypted.expert_notes: Backup vault encrypted (preferably with CMEK). encrypted_at_rest_cmek is stronger check.

backup._note: Backup has 4/12 mapped. Core encryption and retention checks covered.

elbv2.service_description: AWS Elastic Load Balancing v2 - Application/Network Load Balancers

aws.elbv2.deletion_protection.expert_notes: ALB/NLB deletion protection enabled. Direct mapping.

aws.elbv2.ssl_listeners.expert_notes: Check if ALB uses HTTPS/TLS listeners. protocol_https_or_tls checks Protocol. May also need to verify NO HTTP listeners exist.

aws.elbv2.logging_enabled.expert_notes: ELBv2 access logs. Per CloudTrail principle, API calls captured. For actual access logs, needs ELB access logging attribute check.

elbv2._note: ELBv2 has minimal rule coverage. Most checks need development.

elb.service_description: AWS Elastic Load Balancing - Classic Load Balancers

aws.elb.ssl_listeners.expert_notes: Classic ELB uses HTTPS/SSL listeners. secure_listener_protocols checks this.

aws.elb.logging_enabled.expert_notes: ELB access logging. CloudTrail captures API calls, but access logs (connection logs) need ELB AccessLog attribute check.

elb._note: Classic ELB being deprecated. Minimal coverage expected.

summary.key_principle: CloudTrail enabled = audit logs for all AWS services
